reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes the informal statement. `[Infinite F] [Field F]` captures "infinite field"; `[AddCommGroup V] [Module F V]` is the standard encoding of a vector space over F; `[Finite ι]` with family `u : ι → Submodule F V` represents "a finite number of subspaces"; `hu : ∀ i, u i ≠ ⊤` expresses that each subspace is proper (standard meaning: not equal to V); and the conclusion `(⋃ i, (u i : Set V)) ≠ ⊤` says the set-theoretic union of their underlying sets is not all of V (since `⊤ : Set V = Set.univ`). The universal quantification over families matches the informal "cannot be" claim. Edge cases (empty ι, trivial V) are handled consistently and do not break the equivalence.
